text.title: Rules, Registration, and AI-Agent Gotchas
text.summary: Mandatory rules for BackgroundService correctness and common LLM mistakes to reject in code review.

example.note: Channel and the queue abstraction are singletons. The BackgroundService is registered via AddHostedService — forgetting this is common; the service compiles but never starts.

antipattern.reason: Creates a silent memory leak under load. Always use Channel.CreateBounded with an explicit capacity and FullMode.

antipattern.description: DbContext or other scoped service injected directly into BackgroundService constructor.
antipattern.reason: BackgroundService is a singleton; scoped services throw at runtime on first use. Resolve per-item inside ExecuteAsync via CreateScope.

antipattern.description: ExecuteAsync without try/catch around the loop body.
antipattern.reason: One transient exception terminates the consumer loop; subsequent items are never processed.
Service compiles and injects fine but never starts — silent runtime bug.

antipattern.description: Multi-replica deploy running BackgroundService on every pod.
antipattern.reason: Leads to double-processing. Gate with leader election or migrate to Hangfire/MassTransit.

rule.statement: Log structured fields per item ({OrderId}) and use ILogger.BeginScope per item for distributed trace correlation.
rule.rationale: Background service logs without correlation IDs make triage in production impossible; structured fields enable filtering by entity ID.
